heading: Process-oriented industrial energy efficiency
label: 6349 Process-oriented industrial energy efficiency
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Energy Policy Act of 1992, and not as part of the Energy Policy and Conservation Act which comprises this chapter.

children.text: the term “covered industry” means the food and food products industry, lumber and wood products industry, petroleum and coal products industry, and all other manufacturing industries specified in Standard Industrial Classification Codes 20 through 39 (or successor classification codes);

children.label: i
children.text: improving energy efficiency;

children.label: ii
children.text: reducing environmental impact; and

children.label: iii
children.text: designing technological improvements to increase competitiveness and achieve cost-effective product quality enhancement;

children.label: B
children.text: the identification of opportunities for improving the energy efficiency of lighting, heating, ventilation, air conditioning, and the associated building envelope; and

children.label: C
children.text: the identification of cost-effective opportunities for using renewable energy technology in the production process and in the systems described in subparagraph (B); and
the term “utility” means any person, State agency (including any municipality), or Federal agency, which sells electric or gas energy to retail customers.

children.label: A
children.text: To promote, through appropriate institutions such as universities, nonprofit organizations, State and local government entities, technical centers, utilities, and trade organizations, the use of energy-efficient technologies in covered industries.

children.label: B
children.text: To establish programs to train individuals (on an industry-by-industry basis) in conducting process-oriented industrial assessments and to encourage the use of such trained assessors.

children.label: C
children.text: To assist utilities in developing, testing, and evaluating energy efficiency programs and technologies for industrial customers in covered industries.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 2 Consultation
children.text: States receiving grants under this subsection shall consult with utilities and representatives of affected industries, as appropriate, in determining the most effective use of such funds consistent with the requirements of paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: pursuant to , has considered and made a determination regarding the implementation of the standards specified in paragraphs (7) and (8) of (with respect to integrated resources planning and investments in conservation and demand management); andsection 2621(a) of title 16section 2621(d) of title 16

children.label: i
children.text: allows utilities to recover the costs prudently incurred in providing process-oriented industrial assessments; and

children.label: I
children.text: process-oriented industrial assessments; and

children.label: II
children.text: financial incentives for implementing energy efficiency improvements.

children.heading: Allocation of funds
children.label: 4 Allocation of funds
children.text: Grants made pursuant to this subsection shall be allocated each fiscal year among States meeting the criteria specified in paragraph (3) who have submitted applications 60 days before the first day of such fiscal year. Such allocation shall be made in accordance with a formula to be prescribed by the Secretary based on each State’s share of value added in industry (as determined by the Census of Manufacturers) as a percentage of the value added by all such States.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary determines that the funds made available to the State during the previous 2 years were used in a manner required under paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: such State demonstrates, in a manner prescribed by the Secretary, utility participation in programs established pursuant to this subsection.

children.heading: Coordination with other Federal programs
children.label: 6 Coordination with other Federal programs
children.text: In carrying out the functions described in paragraph (1), States shall, to the extent practicable, coordinate such functions with activities and programs conducted by the Energy Analysis and Diagnostic Centers of the Department of Energy and the Manufacturing Technology Centers of the National Institute of Standards and Technology.

children.heading: Assessment criteria
children.label: 1 Assessment criteria
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall, by contract with nonprofit organizations with expertise in process-oriented industrial energy efficiency technologies, establish and, as appropriate, update criteria for conducting process-oriented industrial assessments on an industry-by-industry basis. Such criteria shall be made available to State and local government, public utility commissions, utilities, representatives of affected process-oriented industries, and other interested parties.October 24, 1992

children.heading: Directory
children.label: 2 Directory
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a nationwide directory of organizations offering industrial energy efficiency assessments, technologies, and services consistent with the purposes of this section. Such directory shall be made available to State governments, public utility commissions, utilities, industry representatives, and other interested parties.

children.heading: Award program
children.label: 3 Award program
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an annual award program to recognize utilities operating outstanding or innovative industrial energy efficiency technology assistance programs.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 4 Meetings
children.text: In order to further the purposes of this section, the Secretary shall convene annual meetings of parties interested in process-oriented industrial assessments, including representatives of State government, public utility commissions, utilities, and affected process-oriented industries.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.
